text: The surgeon elevates the sigmoid colon over the sacral promontory and begins a medial to lateral dissection, focusing on the total mesorectal excision (TME) procedure.

The surgeon performs a ureter check before isolating the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA). The IMA begins to come into view as the surgeon clears adventitial tissue from the vessel. The lateral dissection is initiated to join the medially dissected plane. The surgeon adjusts the depth of dissection in the retroperitoneum, aiming to reach the sponge positioned near the ureter within the crackly tissue.

The surgeon performs total mesorectal excision (TME) in the adventitial plane, with visible hypogastric nerves prominently displayed. The surgical instruments are positioned through port three, with two left arms and one right arm. The dissection progresses circumferentially down to the levator floor in a narrowed male pelvis, where the tumor is located approximately six centimeters from the anal verge.

The surgeon marks around the anterior section, attempting to enter the plane between Denonvilliers' fascia and the holy plane. The previous posterior dissection aids in facilitating the lateral dissection. The surgeon continues to work circumferentially, avoiding collisions of the robotic arms, and progresses anteriorly beneath the seminal vesicles.

The surgeon continues circumferential dissection around the mesorectum, palpating from below to confirm positioning beneath the tumor and assessing the distal margin. The dissection progresses anteriorly beneath the prostate.

The surgeon encounters difficulty with robotic arm positioning, causing arm four to bang against arm one while crossing to the left side. The vessel sealer is used for improved hemostatic control and efficient tissue division. The dissection follows the mesorectum along the pelvic floor, coning in at the levator floor.

The surgeon cleans the distal rectum of its mesorectum and proceeds to staple the rectum using arm four, achieving a favorable angle for stapling from anterior to posterior. The stapler is positioned at the interrectal ring, and the specimen is exteriorized through arm three. An anvil is placed for distal anastomosis, and the surgeon performs a leak test after diverting the patient.
